summary: Superuser Void Permission System - All tests PASSED. Backend and Frontend E2E testing confirmed: Admin (PIN 100) authorized as Superuser, Waiter (PIN 1234) correctly rejected with 403 error, incorrect PINs return 401 'PIN incorrecto'.

issues: Error message shown as generic 'Error verificando PIN' instead of specific API error 'Este usuario no tiene permisos de gerente'. Consider showing the actual API error detail for better UX.

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/auth/verify-manager with Admin PIN 100 returns authorized=true, is_superuser=true
Backend: POST /api/auth/verify-manager with Waiter PIN 1234 returns 403 'Este usuario no tiene permisos de gerente'
Backend: POST /api/auth/verify-manager with incorrect PIN 77777 returns 401 'PIN incorrecto'
Backend: POST /api/auth/verify-manager with Cashier PIN 4321 returns 403 (non-manager)
Backend: POST /api/auth/verify-manager with Kitchen PIN 9999 returns 403 (non-manager)
Backend: Admin can authorize ANY permission (void_items, reprint_receipt, manage_users, etc.)
Backend: Empty PIN returns 400 'PIN es requerido'
Backend: PIN starting with 0 returns 400 validation error
Frontend: Login with PIN 100 navigates to dashboard
Frontend: Manager Authorization dialog shows 8 circles for PIN display
Frontend: Admin PIN 100 fills 3 circles and VERIFICAR PIN succeeds
Frontend: After Admin verification, shows 'Autorizado por: Admin' with checkmark
Frontend: Reason selection dialog appears after successful authorization
Frontend: Waiter PIN 1234 triggers 403 error and shows error message
Frontend: PIN field is cleared after failed verification attempt
action_items: OPTIONAL: Update OrderScreen.js verifyManagerPin to show specific API error message instead of generic 'Error verificando PIN - intenta de nuevo'
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: Superuser rule implemented correctly - Admin role bypasses all permission checks
auth.py: Returns is_superuser=true for Admin, is_superuser=false for others
auth.py: Properly distinguishes between 401 (bad PIN) and 403 (no manager permissions)
OrderScreen.js: verifyManagerPin correctly sends permission parameter to backend
OrderScreen.js: handleManagerAuth correctly displays Superuser badge in toast
seed_data_creation: Created order on Table 2 with 'Tostones con Queso' item for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Superuser void permission system is fully functional. Admin (PIN 100) can authorize any action with is_superuser=true. Non-manager roles (waiter, cashier, kitchen) are rejected with 403 error. Invalid PINs get 401 error. Test file test_superuser_void_permission.py contains all backend regression tests.

features_verified.admin_superuser_authorization: PASS - Admin PIN 100 returns authorized=true, is_superuser=true
features_verified.waiter_rejection: PASS - Returns 403 'Este usuario no tiene permisos de gerente'
features_verified.incorrect_pin_error: PASS - Returns 401 'PIN incorrecto' for non-existent PINs
features_verified.frontend_auth_dialog: PASS - Shows 8 circles, accepts PIN, displays authorization result
features_verified.frontend_success_flow: PASS - After auth, shows reason selection with 'Autorizado por: Admin'
features_verified.frontend_error_handling: PASS - Shows error and clears PIN on failure
